data.q: What is the primary purpose of Composer in the PHP ecosystem?
o: To manage PHP dependencies and packages
To compile PHP code into bytecode
To provide a built-in web server
To format PHP code according to PSR standards
data.e: Composer is a dependency manager for PHP that allows you to declare, install, and manage libraries your project depends on.

data.q: Which file must be included in a PHP project to enable Composer's autoloading functionality?
data.e: The vendor/autoload.php file is generated by Composer and must be required to automatically load all installed classes and dependencies.

data.q: What does the PSR-4 autoloading standard specify?
o: How to write unit tests for PHP classes
How to map fully-qualified namespaces to file system directories
data.e: PSR-4 defines a standard for mapping namespaces to directory structures, allowing autoloading without requiring a physical file for every class.

data.q: Which Composer command regenerates the autoloader files without updating or reinstalling dependencies?
data.e: The composer dump-autoload command regenerates the vendor/autoload.php files, which is useful when adding new classes or changing autoloading rules.

data.q: In a composer.json file, which key is used to define the mapping of namespaces to directories for PSR-4?
data.e: The 'autoload' key in composer.json is used to define autoloading rules, including PSR-4 namespace-to-directory mappings.

data.q: What is the primary role of the composer.lock file?
o: To record the exact versions of all installed dependencies
To lock the PHP version required for the project
To prevent unauthorized access to the vendor directory
data.e: The composer.lock file records the exact versions of all dependencies installed, ensuring consistent environments across different deployments.

data.q: Which Composer autoloading optimization technique maps all classes to a single array to speed up class resolution?
data.e: The 'classmap' autoloading type scans directories and generates a single array mapping class names to file paths, significantly improving load performance.
